name: ui-ux-pro-max
role: Design-system, contrast, and accessibility enforcement layer. Acts as a quality gate on every visual decision.

top_5_synergies.why: 21st-registry supplies the components; ui-ux-pro-max enforces that you USE them with correct contrast, spacing, and typography. Without the gate, teams ship beautiful components with broken a11y.
top_5_synergies.risk: ui-ux-pro-max may flag 21st.dev components as non-compliant; you'll need to override their tokens.

top_5_synergies.why: Stitch generates designs; ui-ux-pro-max validates them. Closes the loop from prompt → design → quality gate.
top_5_synergies.risk: Stitch output may not match your existing token system; manual remap required.

top_5_synergies.why: ui-ux-pro-max enforces prefers-reduced-motion; GSAP respects it. The combo produces motion that doesn't trigger vestibular issues.
top_5_synergies.risk: Over-eager GSAP timelines can override reduced-motion; gate every animation.

top_5_synergies.why: Charts need contrast + colorblind-safe palettes; ui-ux-pro-max supplies the rules, charts executes.
top_5_synergies.risk: Default chart colors may violate ui-ux-pro-max contrast rules; customize.

top_5_synergies.why: fullstack-dev scaffolds; ui-ux-pro-max enforces design discipline on the scaffold. Prevents 'shadcn soup'.
top_5_synergies.risk: Scaffold defaults may conflict with ui-ux-pro-max tokens; adopt a prefix convention.

name: stitch-design
role: Google Labs design generation — prompt → production-ready design markdown + component spec.

top_5_synergies.why: Stitch generates the spec; 21st-registry supplies the components to match. Prompt → spec → real React components.
top_5_synergies.risk: Stitch may specify components that don't exist in 21st.dev; fall back to custom.

top_5_synergies.why: Stitch generates; ui-ux-pro-max validates. Closes the quality loop.
top_5_synergies.risk: Stitch output may not pass a11y checks; manual fixes needed.

top_5_synergies.why: LLM writes the prompt; Stitch renders the design. Two-stage generation: text → design → code.
top_5_synergies.risk: LLM prompts can be vague; Stitch output mirrors the vagueness.

top_5_synergies.why: Image-gen creates hero assets; Stitch places them in a layout. End-to-end visual production.
top_5_synergies.risk: Image-gen aspect ratios may not match Stitch layouts; resize pipeline needed.

top_5_synergies.why: fullstack-dev hosts the components Stitch specifies. Prompt → spec → scaffold.
top_5_synergies.risk: Stitch output may not align with Next.js App Router conventions; remap.

name: 21st-registry
role: 21st.dev component registry install. Pulls production React components by ID. NOTE: skill name is '21st-registry', NOT '21st-dev-components' — the latter flag fails.

top_5_synergies.why: 21st-registry supplies components; ui-ux-pro-max enforces correct usage. The canonical quality-loop combo.
top_5_synergies.risk: Some 21st.dev components may not meet ui-ux-pro-max contrast rules; override tokens.

top_5_synergies.why: Stitch specifies; 21st-registry supplies. Prompt → spec → real components.
top_5_synergies.risk: Stitch may specify non-existent components; custom build required.

top_5_synergies.why: fullstack-dev scaffolds the host app; 21st-registry fills it with components. Fastest path to a real UI.
top_5_synergies.risk: 21st.dev components may need peer deps (motion, etc.); install all.

top_5_synergies.why: 21st-registry supplies static components; GSAP adds motion. Two-layer composition.
top_5_synergies.risk: 21st.dev components may have their own animations that conflict with GSAP; disable theirs.

top_5_synergies.why: 21st-registry for layout/chrome; charts for data viz. Split responsibility by surface.
top_5_synergies.risk: Chart sizing may not fit 21st.dev card slots; wrap in a sizing container.
